title: /api/tracks in-memory snapshot keyed by master.db mtime
description: Build the full TrackItem list once per master.db mtime; subsequent /api/tracks requests return the snapshot directly. Reduces warm-load p95 from ~6s to ~200ms.
acceptanceCriteria: First /api/tracks call (cold) builds the snapshot and stores it on app.state.tracks_snapshot = {mtime, payload: list[TrackItem]}.
Subsequent calls with the same mtime return the cached payload (no DB query).
Calls after master.db mtime changes rebuild the snapshot lazily.
Memory footprint of the snapshot on 10k tracks is < 50MB.
tests/test_serve_routes.py confirms cold-vs-warm distinction (mock get_mtime; first call hits DB; second does not).

steps.details: serve/deps.py lifespan: app.state.tracks_snapshot = None; app.state.tracks_snapshot_lock = threading.Lock().

Handler: current_mtime = os.path.getmtime(db._db_dir / 'master.db'). With app.state.tracks_snapshot_lock: if app.state.tracks_snapshot is not None and app.state.tracks_snapshot['mtime'] == current_mtime: return app.state.tracks_snapshot['payload']. Else fall through to build path.

Build the TrackItem list using existing SQL pattern (CLAUDE.md notes this is intentional fetch-all-then-filter for SQLCipher perf). After building, with the lock: app.state.tracks_snapshot = {'mtime': current_mtime, 'payload': payload}. Return payload.

If two requests arrive cold simultaneously, both could try to build. Use a 'build-in-progress' flag inside the lock: first one sets _building=True, releases the lock, builds, then re-acquires + stores result. Others detect _building=True and wait on a threading.Event.
/api/tracks?playlist_id=N still filters - but the filter happens on the FULL snapshot in memory (intersection with playlist's content_ids). Snapshot is always the full library.

tests/test_serve_routes.py: mock os.path.getmtime + the build path. First call builds; second returns same payload without hitting build. Concurrent test: 10 simultaneous requests result in only 1 build call.

technicalNotes: Per CLAUDE.md: do not 'optimize' the SQL pattern back to IN(row_ids) - fetch-all-then-filter wins for SQLCipher at ~3k. At 10k the breakpoint may shift; TASK-024 re-evaluates.
Memory estimate: ~50 fields/track * 10k tracks * ~30 bytes avg = ~15MB. Conservative cap is 50MB.
